name: pytest
what_it_does: Standard Python test framework with a simple function-based API, powerful fixture system, and extensive plugin ecosystem. pytest discovers and runs tests with minimal boilerplate — plain assert statements, no test class required. Fixtures provide dependency injection for test setup/teardown. 1000+ plugins extend pytest for coverage (pytest-cov), async (pytest-asyncio), Django, FastAPI, and more.
use_cases: Write Python tests using plain assert statements with informative failure messages without boilerplate classes
Use pytest fixtures for reusable test setup/teardown with dependency injection and scope control (function, class, module, session)
Run FastAPI/Django test suites with pytest-asyncio for async test support and framework-specific plugins
Generate test coverage reports with pytest-cov to track code coverage across the test suite
Use parameterize decorator (@pytest.mark.parametrize) to run the same test with multiple input/output combinations
not_for: Browser/E2E testing — use Playwright, Selenium, or Cypress for end-to-end browser tests
Load testing — use Locust or k6 for performance testing
best_when: You're writing any Python code that needs testing — pytest is the standard Python test runner for unit, integration, and functional tests.
avoid_when: You're using a framework with specific test requirements that conflict with pytest (rare — pytest integrates with essentially everything).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: pytest provides best-in-class assertion failure messages with variable values and diff output. Fixture errors include the fixture call stack. Collection errors identify problematic test files.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Tests should be idempotent by design. Pytest itself is deterministic.
known_agent_gotchas: Test discovery requires files named test_*.py or *_test.py and functions named test_* — files not matching this pattern are silently ignored
pytest fixtures use dependency injection by parameter name — fixture function name must EXACTLY match the parameter name; typos cause 'fixture not found' errors
Async tests require pytest-asyncio and either @pytest.mark.asyncio decorator or asyncio_mode='auto' in pytest.ini — without this, async tests never actually run (they pass trivially)
Session-scoped fixtures cannot depend on function-scoped fixtures — fixture scope violations raise errors; understand the scope hierarchy (session > module > class > function)
conftest.py files provide fixtures to all tests in the same directory and subdirectories — fixtures in conftest.py at the wrong level may not be available where expected
pytest.raises() must be used as context manager — using it as a decorator or in assertion form doesn't work; the body after the exception-raising line is NOT executed

security_score_components.security_notes: Test files may contain credentials for test environments — never commit real credentials. Use pytest fixtures with environment variable secrets. Plugin ecosystem is well-maintained but audit plugins before adding to production test suites.
